heading: Rules and regulations for Army and Navy Hospital
label: 18 Rules and regulations for Army and Navy Hospital
text: The Army and Navy General Hospital at Hot Springs, Arkansas, shall be subject to such rules, regulations, and restrictions as shall be provided by the President of the United States and shall remain under the jurisdiction and control of the Department of the Army.
notes: Amendments1930—Act , provided that the hospital was to be subject to the jurisdiction and control of the Department of War.June 18, 1930

Change of NameDepartment of War designated Department of the Army and title of Secretary of War changed to Secretary of the Army by , . Section 205(a) of act , was repealed by , . Section 1 of act enacted “Title 10, Armed Forces”, which in sections 3010 to 3013 continued Department of the Army under administrative supervision of Secretary of the Army.act July 26, 1947, ch. 343, title II, § 205(a)61 Stat. 501act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 1041, § 5370A Stat. 641July 26, 1947Aug. 10, 1956

Ex. Ord. No. 10272. Delegation of Authority to the Secretary of the ArmyEx. Ord. No. 10272, , 16 F.R. 6711, provided:July 10, 1951By virtue of the authority vested in me by , c. 646, [ The President], and as President of the United States, it is hereby ordered that the Secretary of the Army be, and he is hereby, designated and empowered to exercise the authority vested in the President by , , 121, as amended [this section], to provide rules, regulations, and restrictions with respect to the Army and Navy hospital at Hot Springs, Arkansas: , That the rules, regulations, and restrictions prescribed under the authority of this order shall, so far as feasible, be uniform with those obtaining with respect to other hospitals under the jurisdiction of the Department of the Army, and that those prescribed by the Executive order of , as amended by Executive Order No. 6885 of , shall continue in force and effect until amended, modified, or revoked by the Secretary of the Army in action taken pursuant to this order.section 1 of the act of August 8, 195064 Stat. 419section 301 of Title 3section 1 of the act of June 30, 188222 Stat. 117ProvidedAugust 25, 1892October 23, 1934Harry S. Truman.
